acceptance_criteria.description: Acceptance is verified by this exact shell check in a fresh temporary working directory: `habit add "Drink water" && test "$(cat habits.json)" = '[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":false}]' && test "$(habit list)" = '[ ] 1. Drink water' && habit check 1 && test "$(cat habits.json)" = '[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":true}]'`. `habit add "Drink water"` must stdout exactly `Added habit 1: Drink water`, `habit list` must stdout exactly `[ ] 1. Drink water`, and `habit check 1` must stdout exactly `Checked habit 1: Drink water`, each with exit code `0` and empty stderr. `habit check 999` must exit code `1`, stderr exactly `Error: habit not found`, stdout empty, and leave `./habits.json` unchanged. `habit delete 1` must exit code `1`, print an unsupported-command error to stderr, print nothing to stdout, and leave `./habits.json` unchanged

constraints: Keep the implementation to the smallest safe MVP that satisfies the task goal

Non-goal: Non-goals: no cloud sync, paid services, authentication, production deployment, multi-user support, dates/streaks/reminders, edit/delete commands, custom file paths, config files, sorting/filtering/search, colored output, interactive prompts, or commands beyond `habit add "<habit text>"`, `habit list`, and `habit check <id>`. Tests must reject scope expansion by confirming `habit delete 1` exits with code `1`, prints an unsupported-command error to stderr, prints nothing to stdout, and leaves `./habits.json` unchanged

evaluation_principles.description: All required Seed Draft Ledger sections are resolved before execution.
evaluation_principles.name: ledger_completeness

evaluation_principles.description: Run this exact verification in a fresh temporary working directory: `habit add "Drink water" && test "$(cat habits.json)" = '[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":false}]' && test "$(habit list)" = '[ ] 1. Drink water' && habit check 1 && test "$(cat habits.json)" = '[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":true}]'`. Also verify failures with `habit check 999` expecting exit code `1`, stdout empty, stderr exactly `Error: habit not found`, and unchanged `./habits.json`; and `habit delete 1` expecting exit code `1`, stdout empty, an unsupported-command error on stderr, and unchanged `./habits.json`.
evaluation_principles.name: observable_verification

Drink water' && habit check 1 && test "$(cat habits.json)" = '[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":true}]'`. Also verify failures with `habit check 999` expecting exit code `1`, stdout empty, stderr exactly `Error: habit not found`, and unchanged `./habits.json`; and `habit delete 1` expecting exit code `1`, stdout empty, an unsupported-command error on stderr, and unchanged `./habits.json`.

initial_seed_before_repair.goal: Build a small habit-tracker CLI that lets the user add, list, and check off habits, persisting them as JSON in the working directory.

ontology_schema.description: Auto-synthesized ontology from the completed Seed Draft Ledger.

fields.description: One actor: a single local terminal user running `habit` from the working directory. Inputs are CLI commands `habit add "Drink water"`, `habit list`, and `habit check 1`; outputs are stdout text plus a `habits.json` file in the current directory. For a fresh directory, `habit add "Drink water"` prints `Added habit 1: Drink water`, `habit list` prints `[ ] 1. Drink water`, and `habit check 1` prints `Checked habit 1: Drink water`; invalid IDs print `Error: habit not found` to stderr and exit with code `1`.
fields.name: actors

fields.description: Inputs are exactly three CLI forms: `habit add "<habit text>"`, `habit list`, and `habit check <id>`, run by one local terminal user from the working directory. `habit add "Drink water"` creates or updates `./habits.json` and prints `Added habit 1: Drink water`; `habit list` prints `[ ] 1. Drink water`; `habit check 1` marks habit id `1` checked and prints `Checked habit 1: Drink water`. Invalid IDs print `Error: habit not found` to stderr and exit with code `1`.
fields.name: inputs

fields.description: Outputs are stdout text plus a `habits.json` file in the current directory. For a fresh directory, `habit add "Drink water"` prints `Added habit 1: Drink water`, writes `./habits.json` as `[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":false}]`, `habit list` prints `[ ] 1. Drink water`, and `habit check 1` prints `Checked habit 1: Drink water` and updates `./habits.json` to `[{"id":1,"text":"Drink water","checked":true}]`. Invalid IDs print `Error: habit not found` to stderr, print nothing to stdout, leave `./habits.json` unchanged, and exit with code `1`.
fields.name: outputs

fields.description: Use Node.js 20+ with a single executable CommonJS file `bin/habit` using only built-in modules `fs` and `path`, no external dependencies, and `package.json` exposing `"bin": {"habit":"bin/habit"}`. Persist to `path.join(process.cwd(), "habits.json")` as compact JSON with `JSON.stringify(habits)`, read missing files as `[]`, assign IDs as `max(existing ids)+1`, and print all errors to stderr with exit code `1`. Unsupported commands such as `habit delete 1` must print exactly `Error: unsupported command` to stderr, stdout empty, exit code `1`, and leave `./habits.json` unchanged.
fields.name: runtime_context

ontology_schema.name: BuildASmallHabit

question_history.answer: [from-auto][conservative_default] Proceed with a conservative MVP: keep scope small, prefer existing project patterns, document assumptions, and make completion verifiable with observable acceptance criteria.
